description: The screen displays instructions for the user to click the screen sharing button and choose an option to share the window. The cursor is stationary.
frame_description: Dark grey screen with white text instructions in the center, browser tabs visible at the top, and a small video feed of the user in the top right.

description: The instructions on the screen change to 'It looks like your screen share is working.'
frame_description: Dark grey screen with white text 'It looks like your screen share is working.' in the center, browser tabs visible at the top, and a small video feed of the user in the top right.

description: The instructions on the screen update to include the task details: 'Here is your task: Imagine you need to book a return domestic flight within Australia for an upcoming trip. Using the Flight Centre website, search for flights, select your preferred options, and proceed through the booking flow including reviewing any ancillaries and seat selection. Please think out loud as you go - share what you're looking at, what you're thinking, and any reactions you have. You can stop once you reach the page asking for traveller details (you don't need to enter any personal information or complete the actual purchase).'
frame_description: Dark grey screen with white text instructions for the task in the center, browser tabs visible at the top, and a small video feed of the user in the top right.

description: User clicks the 'Open' button to proceed to the Flight Centre website.
frame_description: Dark grey screen with 'Open' button in the center, task instructions above it.

description: The browser navigates to the Flight Centre website.
frame_description: The Flight Centre website loads, showing a red header, a 'Tropical Islands Sale' banner with two people on a beach, and a flight search form.

description: User clicks on the 'Where to?' input field in the flight search form.
frame_description: The Flight Centre homepage is visible with the flight search form. The 'Where to?' input field is now active, displaying a dropdown list of popular Australian cities.

description: User selects 'Melbourne, Australia' from the dropdown list of popular destinations.
frame_description: The Flight Centre homepage is visible. 'Melbourne, Australia' is now populated in the 'Where to?' field, and the dropdown list has disappeared.

description: User clicks on the date input field to open the calendar.
frame_description: The Flight Centre homepage is visible. A calendar pop-up for 'Travel dates' is displayed, showing February and March 2025.

description: User hesitates while viewing the calendar modal for travel dates.
frame_description: Calendar modal is open, showing February and March 2020.

description: User hovers over multiple dates in the March 2020 calendar, specifically March 5, 11, 18, 20, 21, and 22.

description: User selects March 19, 2025, from the calendar.

description: User selects March 22 as the return date.

description: User hesitates before clicking the 'Done' button on the calendar modal.

description: User clicks the 'Done' button on the calendar to confirm the selected dates.
frame_description: The Flight Centre homepage is visible. The calendar pop-up closes, and the selected dates are now displayed in the date input field.

description: User hovers over the 'Done' button.
frame_description: Calendar modal is open, cursor over the 'Done' button.

description: User clicks the 'Done' button to confirm date selection.
frame_description: Calendar modal disappears.
